original: Kevin Eastman, American comic book creator (co-created  Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles), born in Portland, Maine
American motorcycle daredevil Robert "Evel"Knieveljumps 16 automobiles on his motorcycle in Gardena, California
Mel Blanc, American radio comedian and voice actor, best known for his Looney Tunes and Merrie Melodies cartoons (Bugs Bunny, Elmer Fudd & Porky Pig), born in San Francisco, California (d. 1989)
Animated fish tale "Finding Nemo", directed by Andrew Stanton, and starring Albert Brooks andEllen DeGenerespremieres in US and Canada
US Mariner 9 1st satellite to orbit Mars launched
Claudia Alexander, Canadian-born American NASA Scientist (led Galileo mission to Jupiter), born in Vancouver, British Columbia (d. 2015)
Helen Patricia Sharman, British chemist, cosmonaut, and first Western European woman in space (Soyuz TM-12, TM-11), born in Sheffield, England
Hershey Park, a family leisure playground founded byMilton S. Hersheyfor the exclusive use of his employees, opens in Hershey, Pennsylvania
Ted Coombs begins a 5,193 mile roller skate from LA to NYC
Barry Clifford, American underwater explorer (discovered 1st verified pirate shipwreck the Whydah), born in Hyannis, Massachusetts
US launches Surveyor 1 to Moon
Shedd Aquarium, one of the first inland aquariums in the world, opens in Chicago, Illinois
William G. Young of Baltimore, Maryland, receives a patent for “An Improvement in Ice-Cream Freezers”
Joseph W Kennedy, American chemist (Manhattan Project; 1 of 4 discoverers of plutonium), and educator, born in Nacogdoches, Texas (d. 1957)
Peter DePaolo became 1st man to average over 100 mph at Indy
Guanyu Zhou, Chinese racing driver (first Chinese F1 driver), born in Shanghai, China
Peter Carl Fabergé, Russian goldsmith and jeweler (famous for Fabergé eggs), born in Saint Petersburg, Russia (d. 1920)
The Beatles' "Love Me Do" single goes #1 in the United States
The Beatles release single "Paperback Writer" / "Rain" in US; "Rain" featured experimental studio tricks of slowed down bass and drums tracks, and backwards vocals in the fade out
Mickey Mantlemisses by 18 inches hitting 1st home run out of Yankee Stadium in the first game of a doubleheader against the Washington Senators
Boston Beaneaters baseball second baseman Bobby Lowe first to hit 4 home runs in MLB game in 20-11 win against Cincinnati Reds
Walter Johnsonrecords 110th and final shutout of his Baseball HOF career, the most in MLB history; Washington Senators score 3-0 win over Boston Red Sox

Stanley Cup Final, Northlands Coliseum, Edmonton, AL: Edmonton Oilers take back-to-back titles; beat Philadelphia Flyers, 8-3 for a 4-1 series victory;Wayne GretzkyandJari Kurrieach post a goal and 3 assists
Margaret Cavendish is the first woman to attend a meeting of the Royal Society, watching demonstrations by Robert Boyle andRobert Hooke
European Space Agency (ESA) forms
Beatles begin work on their only double album "The Beatles" - popularly known as "The White Album"
The Beatles 1961 recording of an original instrumental "Cry for a Shadow" is #1 in Australia; it is the only song credited to songwriting team ofGeorge HarrisonandJohn Lennon
The Auckland Harbour Bridge is officially opened in Auckland, New Zealand.
Ezzard Charlesbeats Joey Maxim in 15 for heavyweight boxing title
Mike Tysonbeats Pinklon Thomas by TKO in round 6 in Las Vegas to retain WBC / WBA heavyweight boxing titles
Philadelphia pitcher Jim Bivin retiresBabe Ruthon an infield grounder in "the Babe's" final MLB at-bat; Ruth plays just 1 inning in Boston Braves, 11-6 loss to Phillies at the Baker Bowl
Vivian Malone is 1st black to graduate from University of Alabama
Argentinian authorGabriel García Márquez's most important work "One Hundred Years of Solitude" is published in Buenos Aires
Paris advocate E Archdeacon is 1st passenger in a airplane
92°F highest temperature ever recorded in Cleveland in May
Ariane-2 (ESA) launched
The North American Philips Company unveils the compact disc video format (CD-V), which can hold up to 800 MB, enough for a full-length movie
1,047 bombers bomb Cologne in RAF's raid of WW II
The Rideau Canal in eastern Ontario is opened. Oldest continuously operated canal system in North America.
Super Rugby Final, Eden Park, Auckland: In an all-NZ final, Andrew Mehrtens lands 2 penalties & 2 conversions as the Canterbury Crusaders beat the Blues, 20-13; Crusaders' first title
Super Rugby Final, Pretoria: Morné Steyn kicks 5 conversions & 2 penalties as the Bulls thump the Chiefs (Waikato, NZ), 61-17
Test Cricket debut for Rohan Kanhai v England at Edgbaston
James A. Parsons, American metallurgist and inventor (stainless steel), born in Dayton, Ohio (d. 1989)[1]
Countee Cullen, African American poet of the Harlem Renaissance (The Black Christ; Copper Sun), born in Baltimore, Maryland (d. 1946)[1]
Joseph Stein, American dramatist and playwright (Fiddler on the Roof), born in New York City (d. 2010)
Hal Clement [Harry Clement Stubbs], American sci-fi writer (Mission of Gravity; Needle), born in Somerville, Massachusetts (d. 2003)
George Zweig, Russian-American physicist (Quark model), born in Moscow, Soviet Union
Candy Lightner, American political activist/founder (MADD), born in Pasadena, California
Peter Townend, Australian surfer (first IPS/ASP World Champion 1976; International Surfing HOF), born in Coolangatta, Australia
Allison Roe, New Zealand athlete (NYC Marathon, Boston 1981; Seoul 1982), born in Auckland, New Zealand
Michael E Lopez-Alegria, Lt Cmdr/astro (STS 73, sk 92), born in Madrid, Spain
Fernando Amorsolo, Filipino painter nicknamed the "Grand Old Man of Philippine Art", born in Manila, Philippines, Spanish Empire (d. 1972)
Norbert Schemansky, American weightlifter (Olympic gold heavyweight 1952, silver 1948, bronze 1960, 64), born in Detroit, Michigan (d. 2016)
Gladys Horton, American pop singer (The Marvelettes - "Please Mr. Postman"; "Too Many Fish In The Sea"), born in Gainesville, Florida (d. 2011) [some sources have birth year as1944]
Nicky "Topper" Headon, English drummer (Clash. 1977-82 - "London Calling", "Rock the Casbah"), born in Bromley, England
Marie Fredriksson, Swedish pop singer (Roxette - "Must Have Been Love"), born in Össjö, Sweden (d. 2019)
Omri Hairi Katz, actor (Marshall Teller-Eerie Indiana), born in Los Angeles, California
Unidentified soldiers killed in WW II & Korean War buried in Arlington
